answer:
In Mirza Ghalib's *Diwan-e-Ghalib*, the term *fanaa* (فنا)—appearing in transliterated form as "fana" or "fana ho jaana"—evokes both literal destruction (e.g., erasure, fading, or consumption of the self amid worldly pain, love, or transience) and mystical self-annihilation (e.g., ecstatic dissolution into a greater unity, often symbolized by natural metaphors like a drop merging with the river or dew evaporating in the sun, implying union with the divine or absolute).[^1][^2][^3] This duality aligns with Sufi philosophy, where *fanaa* denotes the ego's obliteration for *baqa* (subsistence in God), but Ghalib secularizes and sensualizes it through *ishq* (love), blending profane longing with spiritual rapture.

### Ordinary Destruction (Transience, Pain, Erasure)
Ghalib often deploys *fanaa* for the mundane annihilation of the lover's existence under separation (*firāq*), unrequited desire, or life's ephemerality—depicting self-loss as a grinding, destructive diminishment rather than transcendence:
- The lover's being is "erased" (*fana*) until only a hollow oath remains, proving human existence is inherently self-destroying.[^4]
- Cries of grief "consumed themselves" in fear of the beloved's disdain, a futile wasting away.[^5]
- The heart's struggles wear away like a knot rubbed to oblivion, evoking mechanical breakdown.[^6]

These senses emphasize *fanaa* as ordinary ruin: the body's frailty, love's attrition, and mortality's inexorable fade.

### Mystical Self-Annihilation (Blissful Merger, Ecstasy)
Conversely, Ghalib activates *fanaa*'s Sufi core in ecstatic union, where destruction yields rapture through metaphors of liquid or fragile elements dissolving into vastness (river, sun, furnace)—the self's annihilation as joyful surrender:
- The iconic opening: "A drop's true bliss [*ishrat*] is in the river to become [*fana ho jaana*]," where the drop's merger symbolizes ego-loss as supreme pleasure.[^7]
- Dewdrops "vanish" in the sun's majesty, mirroring the poet's self-loss before Divine Names—a Sufi *fanaa fi-Allah* yielding "fearsome ecstasy."[^8][^9]
- Straw finds "glory" in the furnace: self-annihilation as the path to truth.[^10]

Ghalib toggles these via context and imagery: destructive in love's torment (e.g., chapters 18, 46), mystical in cosmic metaphors (e.g., chapters 3, 86, 96). No single work exhausts the concept, but ghazal 86 paradigmatically unites them, equating pain's excess with healing dissolution.[^11]

[^1]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, ch. 86 ["Ishrat-e-qatra hai darya mein fana ho jaana"](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/86-ishrat-e-qatra-hai-darya-mein-fana-ho-jaana/translation/#p-a9ff31).

[^2]: Ibid., [full opening couplet](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/86-ishrat-e-qatra-hai-darya-mein-fana-ho-jaana/translation/#p-a9ff31).

[^3]: Ibid., ch. 46 ["Garm-faryaad rakha shakl-e-nihaali ne mujhe"](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/46-garm-faryaad-rakha-shakl-e-nihaali-ne-mujhe/translation/#p-6b73ab).

[^4]: Ibid., ch. 18 ["Be-e'tedaaliyon se subuk sab mein hum huay"](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/18-be-etedaaliyon-se-subuk-sab-mein-hum-huay/translation/#p-da73dd).

[^5]: Ibid., ch. 18, [maqta](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/18-be-etedaaliyon-se-subuk-sab-mein-hum-huay/translation/#p-9b92c9).

[^6]: Ibid., ch. 86, [couplet on heart's knot](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/86-ishrat-e-qatra-hai-darya-mein-fana-ho-jaana/translation/#p-57cff3).

[^7]: Ibid., ch. 86, [opening radif](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/86-ishrat-e-qatra-hai-darya-mein-fana-ho-jaana/translation/#p-a9ff31).

[^8]: Ibid., ch. 46, [closing couplet](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/46-garm-faryaad-rakha-shakl-e-nihaali-ne-mujhe/translation/#p-6b73ab).

[^9]: Ibid., ch. 3 ["Aah ko chahiye ik umr asar hote tak"](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/03-aah-ko-chahiye-ik-umr-asar-hote-tak/translation/#p-9ed8d2).

[^10]: Ibid., ch. 96 ["Junoon ki dastgiri kis se ho gar ho na uryaani"](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/96-junoon-ki-dastgiri-kis-se-ho-gar-ho-na-uryaani/translation/#p-bb2d27).

[^11]: Ibid., ch. 86, [multi-couplet on pain-to-cure](https://falsafa-corpus.com/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/86-ishrat-e-qatra-hai-darya-mein-fana-ho-jaana/translation/#p-0512f8).
